text.title: sec-secrets-defense-in-depth Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for sec-secrets-defense-in-depth as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Adding `*.env`, `*.pem`, `credentials.json` to `.gitignore` and considering the problem solved.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: `.gitignore` only protects untracked files; once a secret is committed it stays in history regardless. Combine `.gitignore` with the hook plus a gitleaks allowlist.

failure-mode.description: Running TruffleHog non-verified and adding everything it finds to the dismiss list.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The dismiss list grows until real secrets land and are auto-suppressed; pairing diff-scope with `--results=verified` gives you a list that stays small and meaningful.

failure-mode.description: Rotating the secret silently, without logging the issuer's confirmation ID, "to avoid embarrassment."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Future audits cannot prove revocation; SOC2 and ISO 27001 control evidence requires the confirmation artifact.
